title: SparkPost API keys should not be disclosed
description: In most cases, trust boundaries are violated when a secret is exposed in a source code repository or an uncontrolled deployment environment. Unintended people who don’t need to know the secret might get access to it. They might then be able to use it to gain unwanted access to associated services or resources.
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

checks.message: SparkPost API key detected in source code. SparkPost is a cloud-based email delivery platform that provides transactional and marketing email services through SMTP relay and REST API. This API key provides programmatic access to send emails, manage email templates, access delivery analytics, configure webhooks, manage recipient lists, and control sending domains and IP pools. Exposing this API key allows attackers to: send unauthorized emails through your SparkPost account potentially for spam or phishing campaigns damaging your sender reputation and causing domain/IP blacklisting, exhaust your email sending quota causing service disruption to legitimate transactional emails like password resets and order confirmations, access sensitive email delivery data including recipient lists, engagement metrics (opens, clicks, bounces), and customer email addresses violating privacy regulations (GDPR, CCPA), modify email templates to inject malicious content or phishing links, manage suppression lists revealing bounced addresses and complaints, steal customer contact databases and recipient lists, incur significant financial costs through excessive email sending, compromise sending domain authentication settings (SPF, DKIM, DMARC), access webhook configurations to intercept email event data, manage subaccounts potentially creating additional compromised credentials, and access transmission and message event data revealing user behavior patterns. IMMEDIATELY revoke this API key from SparkPost dashboard, generate a new replacement key with minimal required permissions, audit recent API activity for unauthorized sends, and store API keys securely using environment variables or secret management systems.
